title: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 23, 2026

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 23, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of the minutes for the Planning Board meeting held on March 23, 2026.

Please provide any minutes for this meeting, whether in draft or final form.

If no minutes exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

This request includes any records that document the discussions, deliberations, and actions taken at the March 23, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

For context, I note that the Planning Board subsequently met on April 6, April 27, May 4, and May 18, 2026. At the May 18, 2026 meeting, the Board approved the March 9, 2026 meeting minutes.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eileen Kelleher

title: Miami Beach PD Accurint/PSDEX Data Sharing

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) What Miami Beach PD systems are shared with LexisNexis Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC) or Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX)? Typically this includes CAD, RMS, Jail Booking systems, etc.
- Please Provide: A brief written description will suffice - however if that is not possible, please provide a document from the integration process that shows which system(s) contribute to AVCC/PSDEX
2) What data is contributed to AVCC/PSDEX - does it include 911 caller information or incident witness/victim information? 
- Please Provide: A brief written description will suffice. If that is not possible, please provide a specific API/XML doc or other technical specifications that show which data fields your agency contributes to AVCC/PSDEX. 
3) Are you able to audit how your agency's data is used by other agencies once it enters AVCC/PSDEX? 
- Please Provide: A short written description, however if that is not possible, please provide an audit log of other AVCC subscriber's searches of Miami Beach PD's data. Any 1-week period is sufficient. 

The following documents have already been reviewed and do not answer the above questions (they are being provided for context only):
A) City Council Agenda: September 21, 2020: https://d2kbkoa27fdvtw.cloudfront.net/cityftmyers/e5f6e0e05c1db33a49c44224412ded210.pdf
B) Information Sharing Agreement: https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/attachment/pdf/691623/FDLE_Interagency_Agreement_-_LexisNexis_-_Accurint_Virtual_Crime_Center_-_LCA_and_CA__2019__-_FMPD.pdf
C) LexisNexis Terms & Conditions: https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/attachment/pdf/691621/20190718014704_lnrsMasterTCsGovt_-_Fort_Myers.pdf
D) AVCC Subscription Schedule A (Fort Meyers): https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/attachment/pdf/691622/AVCC_Schedule_A_-_Ft_Myers_PD_071719.pdf 
E) AVCC Addendum (Florida): https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/attachment/pdf/691620/20190718014704_AVCC_FLORIDA_Addendum_Fort_Myers.pdf

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

title: Flock Safety FOIA

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Flock Safety FOIA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to the following records related to the Winston-Salem Police Department's Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) system:

1. Organization Audit in Flock. The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock. The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization's Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Data Sharing:
   a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the department shares ALPR data ("Flock networks shared with me")
   b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the department receives ALPR data
   c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the department shares hotlist information ("Hotlists that I'm sharing")
   d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the department receives hotlist information ("Hotlists shared with me")

4. All current contracts, MOUs, statements of work, purchase orders, and amendments between the City of Winston-Salem/WSPD and Flock Safety (Flock Group, Inc.) and/or Axon Enterprise, Inc. related to ALPR systems.

5. The written ALPR use policy adopted pursuant to N.C. Gen. Stat. § 20-183.32, and any related SOPs or general orders governing ALPR data access, retention, and sharing.

6. Semi-annual ALPR audit reports generated by the ALPR Program Manager, as referenced in the WSPD ALPR FAQ.

Please provide audit data in a structured, machine-readable format such as .csv or .xlsx. Per N.C. Gen. Stat. § 132-6.2(a), I elect to receive all records in their original electronic format. Do not produce physical copies.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Peircen Lorenz

Sargent for the position of Police Officer, as set out in a conditional offer letter dated September 18, 2017 (for example, whether it resulted in a formal offer, was rescinded, lapsed with no final offer, or was declined or withdrawn by Mr. Sargent).

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Based on the February 12, 2024 City Council meeting minutes, it appears that Lathrop PD was authorized to enter into a "Memorandum of Understanding with the San Joaquin
County Sheriff and local law enforcement agencies to create a public safety data exchange Consortium". - We are requesting a copy of that signed MOU.

6) Beyond the data contained in the link below, we are requesting any additional files, communications, or presentation material (if any) made for the February 12, 2024 City Council Meeting related to the sharing of Lathrop PD data with LexisNexis and other local, state, and federal AVCC subscribers.

title: U.S. Responses to Foreign State-Sponsored Health Disinformation

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: U.S. Responses to Foreign State-Sponsored Health Disinformation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All memoranda, assessments, and analytical reports addressing foreign state-sponsored health disinformation campaigns, including to disinformation related to COVID-19 vaccines, pandemic origins, or public health measures, attributed to or associated with the Russian Federation or Internet Research Agency (Агентство интернет-исследований) or the People's Republic of China, from January 1, 2020 through December 31, 2023.

All records reflecting U.S. diplomatic responses to identified foreign state-sponsored health disinformation, including any formal protests, demarches, or multilateral coordination efforts.

Policy frameworks, guidelines, or internal strategies governing the U.S. government's response to foreign state-sponsored health disinformation, including records that address the distinction between countering foreign disinformation and restrictions on domestic speech.

Records of interagency coordination, including with the NSC, DHS, HHS, and the intelligence community, regarding attribution of health disinformation to foreign state actors and the formulation of responsive measures.

Communications with NATO and the EU regarding coordinated responses to foreign health disinformation campaigns.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: State Dept  - US positions in WHO negotiations on health information (U.S. Department of State)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Memoranda, briefing documents, and internal analyses mentioning U.S. negotiating positions on provisions relating to "health information", "health misinformation", "misinformation",  "disinformation", or the "infodemic" in:
   a. Negotiations over amendments to the International Health Regulations (IHR), from January 1, 2022 through December 31, 2024;
   b. Negotiations over the WHO Pandemic Accord (also referred to as the Pandemic Treaty or CA+), from January 1, 2021 through December 31, 2024.

Interagency communications, including communications with HHS, NSC, USTR, and the White House, regarding U.S. positions on health information-related provisions in the above instruments.

All records reflecting U.S. responses to, or assessments of, proposed treaty or IHR language that would create state obligations related to countering health misinformation or disinformation, including any legal analyses of how such obligations would interact with U.S. domestic law, including the First Amendment.

Records of any U.S. statements, interventions, or objections made at WHO Intergovernmental Negotiating Body (INB) sessions or IHR Review Committee meetings regarding information-related provisions, from January 1, 2021 through December 31, 2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

title: ALPR Information

communications.subject: New Jersey Open Public Records Act Request: ALPR Information
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records concerning Mount Olive Township’s automated license plate reader/recognition systems, including any fixed, mobile, or vehicle-mounted ALPR cameras and related software, portals, databases, cloud services, integrations, or services provided by Packetalk / Packettalk or any reseller, successor, subcontractor, or related vendor.

For records below, please search from January 1, 2024 through the date this request is processed, and include earlier records if they relate to the initial evaluation, procurement, approval, installation, or configuration of the currently used or planned ALPR system. This includes, but is not limited to, records connected to the Township Council’s 2024 and 2025 actions concerning automated license plate readers, including Resolution 160-2025 and Resolution 263-2025.

Please provide the following:

1. Procurement and approval records
All contracts, purchase orders, invoices, quotes, proposals, bids, cooperative purchasing/state contract documents, statements/scopes of work, renewal documents, amendments, funding/grant records, council resolutions, memoranda, and approval materials concerning Packetalk/Packettalk ALPR systems.

2. Camera location, installation, and deployment records 
Records sufficient to show the current and historical locations of ALPR cameras; installation dates; removal/relocation dates; site plans, maps, diagrams, work orders, permits, right-of-way approvals, utility/network records, and any agreements with private businesses, homeowner associations, property owners, or other non-government entities concerning installation, access, or use of ALPR cameras or data.

3. Policies, procedures, training, and controls  
All policies, SOPs, memoranda, training materials, user manuals, administrative guides, presentations, access-control rules, acceptable-use policies, data-sharing policies, hotlist policies, audit-review procedures, disciplinary/enforcement procedures, privacy policies, and retention/deletion policies concerning ALPR use.

4. Data collected and retention 
Records sufficient to show what ALPR data is collected or stored, including available fields such as plate number, timestamp, camera/location, image, vehicle characteristics, confidence score, hit/alert status, user notes, or other metadata. Please include records showing retention periods, deletion/purge schedules, backups, storage locations, vendor-hosted systems, and any changes to retention settings.

5. Aggregate collection and alert statistics
Reports or exports showing the aggregate number of plate reads/detections, hotlist hits/alerts, searches, and/or queries by month, camera, or system-wide total. If no monthly report exists, please provide the closest available dashboard export, statistical report, or database export.

6. Audit logs and user activity logs
All audit, event, access, search, query, organization, network, download, export, sharing, hit-review, or administrative logs for the ALPR system. Please provide these in native electronic format such as CSV, XLS/XLSX, database export, or other machine-readable format where available. Responsive fields include date/time, user/account, agency, action type, search/query terms or rules, access reason/case number if recorded, camera/data searched, records exported/downloaded, and any sharing or external-access events. If the system does not record access reasons or certain fields, please indicate that no responsive record exists for that field.

7. Users and permissions  
Records sufficient to show all Mount Olive Township or Mount Olive Police Department accounts authorized to access the ALPR system, including account names or identifiers, roles/permission levels, agency/department affiliation, account creation/deactivation dates, administrator status, and access-review records.

8. Hotlists/watchlists
Records sufficient to identify all hotlists/watchlists used with the ALPR system, the source of each list, dates active, who can add/remove entries, alert recipients, and any agreements or policies governing hotlist use.

9. Data sharing and outside access  
Records sufficient to show all agencies, organizations, vendors, or private entities with which Mount Olive shares ALPR data, receives ALPR data, shares hotlist information, or receives hotlist information. Please include MOUs, data-sharing agreements, configuration/export reports, access lists, audit logs of outside-agency searches of Mount Olive ALPR data, and records showing any restrictions or approval requirements.

10. Correspondence 
Emails, letters, memoranda, calendar invitations, meeting notes, and attachments discussing the procurement, purchase, funding, location selection, installation, configuration, use, policies, retention, audit controls, sharing, or privacy/security implications of Packetalk/Packettalk ALPR systems. Please include correspondence between Township/Police personnel and Packetalk/Packettalk representatives, and internal Township/Police correspondence containing terms such as “Packetalk,” “Packettalk,” “ALPR,” “automated license plate,” “license plate reader,” or “plate reader.”

I am not requesting bulk disclosure of individual historical plate-scan images or plate numbers except where such values appear in audit logs or records necessary to show searches, access, sharing, or system use. If any portions are exempt, please redact only the exempt portions and provide the remainder, identifying the statutory basis for each redaction or withholding.

If any responsive records are maintained by another Mount Olive office, IT, Finance, Purchasing, the Township Clerk, the Business Administrator, or a vendor on the Township’s behalf, please forward this request or identify the appropriate custodian.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tim Halbur

title: SOPs, data requirements, and review templates for TSCA biotech environmental-release submissions

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: SOPs, data requirements, and review templates for TSCA biotech environmental-release submissions
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), desk manuals, review handbooks, or equivalent internal guidance documents used by EPA staff to review TSCA Section 5 submissions for microorganisms intended for environmental release or field testing — including TERAs, MCANs, and Tier I/II exemption notices.

Internal checklists, data requirements tables, or templates identifying the categories of information EPA requests or expects from submitters in connection with environmental-release submissions.

Risk assessment frameworks, templates, or guidance documents used to evaluate environmental-release submissions, including any documents describing how human health, ecological, and environmental risks are characterized for engineered microorganisms.

Templates used to draft consent orders, Significant New Use Rules (SNURs), or "not unreasonable risk" determinations for biotech submissions involving environmental release.
Internal training materials prepared for new reviewers in the Biotechnology Program.

I am not requesting case-specific risk assessments or submitter-specific records in this request — only the generic procedural, methodological, and training documents.
Date range: January 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed.
Format: Electronic copies in original format (PDF, Word, Excel). Please provide records on a rolling basis as they become available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty

title: Index of TERA, MCAN, and Tier I/II biotech submissions for environmental release, FY2021–present

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Index of TERA, MCAN, and Tier I/II biotech submissions for environmental release, FY2021–present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list, index, log, tracking spreadsheet, or equivalent record identifying all of the following submissions received by EPA where the submission concerns environmental release, field testing, or any intended use of the microorganism outside of contained structures:

TSCA Experimental Release Applications (TERAs)
Microbial Commercial Activity Notices (MCANs)
Tier I exemption notices
Tier II exemption notices

For each submission, I request the following non-CBI fields to the extent maintained:

Case number or docket identifier
Submission title or short description
Date of receipt
Submitter name (where not designated as CBI)
Type of submission (TERA, MCAN, Tier I, or Tier II)
Status or disposition (e.g., under review, withdrawn, dropped from review, completed, expired)
Date of final EPA action, if any
Intended use category or organism type, where captured in tracking records

I am specifically excluding submissions limited to contained use (e.g., fermentor-based industrial production with no environmental release component).
Date range: October 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed.
If no single tracking record exists, please provide the closest equivalent (e.g., entries exported from the Biotechnology Program's case management system).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty

communications.communication:
Dear Niko  McCarty, 

Thank you for submitting your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request. 
Case Number 2026-EPA-05038 has been assigned to this request. In all future correspondence regarding this request, please reference your case number. 
If you selected EPA National FOIA Office (for all non-OIG requests), your request was transmitted directly to the EPA’s National FOIA Office (NFO). NFO will confirm receipt and assignment of your request by sending you an Acknowledgement email within five business days. 
If you have any questions, please contact the FOIA Requester Service Center at hq.foia@epa.gov (mailto:hq.foia@epa.gov) or at 202-566-1667.
Regards, 
EPA National FOIA Office

title: Correspondence Between DOS and ALPR Vendors

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Correspondence Between DOS and ALPR Vendors
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am looking for a few pieces of information related to:
CR26-0246, a contract between the city of Denver and Axon Enterprise Inc. allowing for the purchase and install of multiple ALPR Units (https://denver.legistar.com/LegislationDetail.aspx?ID=7938541&GUID=093F64CB-3951-4C69-9B4B-14B57D302CA6&Options=&Search=)
Denver Police Department had a pilot program and then a contract with Flock Safety that the police found very useful. They then cancelled that contract and entered into a new contract with Axon Enterprise Inc. I'm hoping to find out what about the old system or the old vendor police and/or city leadership didn't like, as well as what they do like about this new system. I’d also like to find out what the police hoped to get out of the old system, how they talked about it internally, and what they are hoping to get out of the new system.

Please provide the following: 

1. The planned locations, or current locations of the ALPR Units from contract POLIC-202683606

2. Contract POLIC-202683606

3. Correspondence (emails, memos, instant messages, etc.) sent internally or DPD/DOS employee to Vendors of ALPR’s.
To reduce the burden and scope of this request, please limit correspondence to items dated between Jan 1, 2022 to the time this request is processed.
To further reduce the burden and scope of the request, limit correspondence with ALPR vendors to employees or parties representing Flock Safety and Axon Enterprise, Inc. (for emails: addresses ending in @flocksafety.com; @axon.com)
To further reduce the burden and scope of the request, please return correspondence with subject line or contents including key phrases such as:
Flock
Flock Safety
25-0413
ALPR
LPR
LPR's
Axon ALPR
Axon LPR
CR26-0246
CR 26-0246
CR 26 0246
26-0246
POLIC-202683606
202683606
Surveillance
Surveillance Task Force

Finally, to further limit the burden and scope of this request, limit correspondence to items where the following parties are either a sender or recipient:
Ron Thomas, Chief of Police
Al Gardner, Deputy Mayor
Emily Lauck, Policy Director
Commander Cliff Barnes, Director of DPD Cyber Bureau
Jacob Hererra
Todd Ericson

For email and other applicable mediums of correspondence, please include attachments.

I appreciate the possibility that even after the attempted limiting of scope and burden, there may still be a considerable amount of items that fit this request. Please let me know if this is the case and I will do my best to further refine the scope of the search.

Thank you for your help,
William Beckett
Resident of the Baker Neighborhood, Denver

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Beckett

title: May 14th Budget Meeting Incident

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: May 14th Budget Meeting Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request copies of all public records in the custody of the Rocky Mount Police Department related to an incident that occurred on or about May 14, 2026, at or near Rocky Mount City Hall, during or following a Rocky Mount City Council meeting.

Specifically, I request:

1. All incident reports, police reports, arrest records, and associated documentation related to the altercation on May 14, 2026, at or near City Hall;
2. All body-worn camera footage, surveillance footage, or other video or audio recordings capturing or related to the incident;
3. All dispatch records, CAD logs, and communications related to any calls for service at or near City Hall on May 14, 2026;
4. All security plans, threat assessments, or operational plans for the Rocky Mount City Council meeting held on May 14, 2026; and
5. Any after-action reports, policy reviews, or documentation reflecting security changes implemented or proposed following the incident.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon, and produce all non-exempt portions.

I request that records be provided in electronic format where available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Peter Herring

title: Plymouth Community Preservation Committee / A.D. Makepeace December 7, 2023 record packet

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Plymouth Community Preservation Committee / A.D. Makepeace December 7, 2023 record packet
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request records from the Plymouth Selectmen's Office and/or Community Preservation Committee concerning the Plymouth Community Preservation Committee meeting held on or about December 7, 2023 and any grant, recommendation, vote, application, or agenda item involving A.D. Makepeace Company, Redbrook, Makepeace Farms, Federal Furnace Cranberry, Garden Road, or any Makepeace-affiliated entity.

Please produce:

1. Final and draft minutes for the December 7, 2023 CPC meeting.
2. Complete audio and/or video recording of the meeting.
3. Agenda, agenda packet, staff memorandum, member packet, and all attachments distributed before or during the meeting.
4. All applications, supporting materials, maps, budgets, scoring sheets, evaluation worksheets, recommendation memoranda, and vote records for any A.D. Makepeace / Makepeace-affiliated request considered at or around that meeting.
5. Roll-call votes, recusals, conflict disclosures, abstentions, or correspondence concerning the Makepeace item.
6. Communications from October 1, 2023 through February 29, 2024 between CPC members, Select Board members, Town Manager staff, Planning staff, Conservation staff, A.D. Makepeace representatives, consultants, attorneys, or lobbyists concerning the CPC item.

Search terms: "Makepeace", "A.D. Makepeace", "Redbrook", "Federal Furnace", "Garden Road", "cranberry", "CPC", and "Community Preservation". If any portion is exempt, please identify the specific exemption claimed and produce all segregable portions. Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

Dear  Edward Havey : 

We have received your public Records request for Town of Plymouth. This record is being assigned to appropriate department. The department reviewer will review the request and create an effort estimate to create the response. Per state mandate, for Town of Plymouth, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will create a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will start working on your request. 

In case the estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that state mandate allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. We will do our due diligence towards this effort. 3.    If in the opinion of the reviewer it is determined that original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort, the due date shall be extended based on reviewer’s judgment. You will be promptly notified in such case with revised due date.

title: FOIA: Shineful Energy (U.S. Department of State)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA: Shineful Energy (U.S. Department of State)
communications.communication:
May 19, 2026

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, as amended by the FOIA Improvement Act of 2016, and Treasury's implementing regulations at 31 C.F.R. Part 1, Subpart A, I request the records described in Part III below.

This request is focused. Public reporting based on internal PdVSA data indicates that since July 2025, Shineful Energy — a trading firm controlled by Ramón Carretero, a Panamanian businessperson the U.S. Treasury designated as a Specially Designated National in December 2025 for acting on behalf of the Government of Venezuela and for business dealings with the Maduro-Flores family — has won every contract to export PdVSA's share of crude from the Petroboscán field operated by Chevron, totaling roughly 11 million barrels and approximately $500 million in proceeds. The records sought are those necessary to understand (a) when and how OFAC identified Shineful Energy as a permitted or de facto offtaker for crude attributable to the Chevron-Venezuela license; (b) what due diligence, screening, or notice OFAC performed in connection with that arrangement; (c) what OFAC, the Office of Global Targeting, and Chevron knew about Carretero's relationship to Shineful Energy and to the Maduro-Flores family, and when; and (d) what enforcement action, if any, OFAC has taken in response.

Part I sets out matters already officially acknowledged so as to foreclose a Glomar response. Part II defines key terms. Part III sets out the records sought in priority order. Part IV addresses anticipated exemption claims. Parts V–VIII address search adequacy, format, fees and expedited processing, and procedure.

I. Matters already officially acknowledged
The following are matters of official public acknowledgment by the Department of the Treasury or other authoritative sources and accordingly cannot support a "Glomar" response or categorical Exemption 1 or Exemption 3 withholding under Wolf v. CIA, 473 F.3d 370 (D.C. Cir. 2007), and ACLU v. CIA, 710 F.3d 422 (D.C. Cir. 2013):

On or about July 24, 2025, OFAC issued a specific license authorizing Chevron Corporation to resume operations in Venezuela under conditions prohibiting cash payments to the Government of Venezuela, with payment to the Government of Venezuela to be made in oil rather than cash. The State Department publicly confirmed the existence of this arrangement on or about July 24, 2025.
In or about December 2025, OFAC designated Ramón Carretero as a Specially Designated National. Treasury's accompanying public statement asserted that Mr. Carretero "engaged in lucrative contracts with the Maduro regime and has had various business dealings with the Maduro-Flores family, including partnering in several companies together."

In December 2025, U.S. authorities seized a tanker publicly reported to be tied to Mr. Carretero.

On January 6, 2026, the President publicly stated that under a U.S.-Venezuela energy arrangement, the United States would market and sell Venezuelan oil and deposit proceeds into "U.S.-controlled" accounts, referencing "Sanctioned Oil" reportedly worth approximately $3 billion.

The Congressional Research Service has reported, on the basis of agency confirmation, that "[i]n July 2025, Treasury reportedly issued a specific license allowing Chevron's JVs to resume operations but prohibiting them from making cash payments to the Venezuelan government." CRS, Venezuela: Overview of U.S. Sanctions Policy, IF10715.
The existence of the specific license, the in-kind payment mechanism, Mr. Carretero's connections to the Maduro-Flores family, and Treasury's awareness of him are therefore matters of official acknowledgment.

II. Definitions
"Record" means any item within the meaning of 44 U.S.C. § 3301, including but not limited to final documents, drafts, marginalia, annotations, attachments, metadata, calendar entries, meeting notes (handwritten or electronic), briefing memoranda, talking points, decision memoranda, transmittal sheets, license applications, attestations, audit reports, vessel-tracking records, and visitor or access logs.

"Communication" means any record reflecting an exchange of information, in whatever medium, including email (on any domain), text/SMS, iMessage, Microsoft Teams, Slack, Signal, WhatsApp, voicemails, voicemail transcripts, telephone logs, and any messages on any ephemeral or encrypted messaging application used to conduct agency business. Consistent with the Federal Records Act and Competitive Enterprise Institute v. OSTP, 827 F.3d 145 (D.C. Cir. 2016), communications conducted on personal accounts or personal devices about official business are agency records and are within the scope of this request.

"Custodian" means any officer, employee, detailee, contractor, consultant, or other person acting on behalf of the agency, including any individual serving in a "special government employee" capacity or "acting" capacity, whose records may reasonably be calculated to contain responsive material.

"Shineful Entities" means Shineful Energy and any subsidiary, affiliate, predecessor, successor, alias, or front company associated with Shineful Energy or with Ramón Carretero, including any entity identified in OFAC's December 2025 designation materials.

"Carretero" means Ramón Carretero, a/k/a Ramon Carretero, Carretero Mendoza, and any other identified aliases.

Date range. Unless otherwise specified, January 1, 2025 through the date the agency conducts its search. Items concerning pre-existing OFAC knowledge of Mr. Carretero or the Shineful Entities are not bounded by start date.

III. Records requested (in priority order)
I ask that the agency produce records on a rolling basis in the priority order below.

Priority 1 — Pre-designation and pre-license knowledge of Carretero and Shineful
All records, from any date through July 31, 2025, in OFAC's possession (including OFAC's Office of Global Targeting, Licensing, Compliance and Enforcement, and Chief Counsel) referring or relating to Ramón Carretero, the Shineful Entities, or either's relationship to PdVSA, the Government of Venezuela, or the Maduro-Flores family. This Priority is intended to capture pre-existing intelligence, screening hits, prior license-application screening, prior enforcement referrals, and any analysis identifying Carretero or Shineful as risks before the issuance of the July 2025 Chevron specific license.

Priority 2 — The Carretero designation file
All records supporting OFAC's December 2025 designation of Mr. Carretero, including the action memorandum, evidentiary package, attribution analysis, redacted SDN listing rationale, any inter-agency concurrences, and any communications between OFAC and the Department of Justice, the Department of State, or the intelligence community concerning the designation.

Priority 3 — Identification of Shineful as offtaker
All records reflecting (a) when and how OFAC first became aware that the Shineful Entities had been awarded contracts to export PdVSA's share of crude from the Petroboscán field or any other Chevron-PdVSA joint venture; (b) any communications between OFAC and Chevron, between OFAC and PdVSA-adjacent persons, or among OFAC components concerning the identity of the offtaker(s) for PdVSA's share of the crude; and (c) any approval, no-action position, or interpretive guidance issued by OFAC concerning the offtaker arrangement.

Priority 4 — Chevron compliance submissions referencing Shineful
All compliance reports, attestations, KYC submissions, due-diligence files, beneficial-ownership disclosures, and other records submitted by or on behalf of Chevron Corporation pursuant to the July 2025 specific license (or any predecessor or successor license) that identify or reference the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, the Petroboscán offtake arrangement, or any trading firm awarded PdVSA's share of crude.

Priority 5 — OFAC due diligence on the offtaker arrangement
All internal OFAC due-diligence files, SDN-screening records, Worldcheck or commercial-database queries, beneficial-ownership analyses, and risk assessments concerning the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or the offtaker(s) selected for PdVSA's share of crude from the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures since July 1, 2025.

Priority 6 — Vessel, cargo, and shipping records
All records — including vessel-tracking data, shipping nominations, lifting schedules, bill-of-lading copies, AIS/EE records, and reports submitted by Chevron pursuant to license conditions — identifying any vessel or tanker that has loaded crude attributable to the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures since July 1, 2025, together with the identified consignee, end-buyer, and destination port. Records referencing tankers associated with the Shineful Entities or Mr. Carretero (including the tanker reportedly seized by U.S. authorities in December 2025) are specifically included.

Priority 7 — Records concerning destination and payment
Records sufficient to show (a) the destination jurisdictions for crude exported by the Shineful Entities under the offtake arrangements at issue (including but not limited to exports to the People's Republic of China); (b) the form and channels of payment for that crude, including any reports, intelligence, or analysis concerning the use of cryptocurrency or alternative payment mechanisms; and (c) the destination of proceeds.

Priority 8 — Enforcement records
All records concerning any enforcement action, investigation, license revocation, cease-and-desist, blocking notice, or referral to the Department of Justice related to the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or any vessel or transaction associated with either, including the December 2025 tanker seizure and any related forfeiture proceedings.

To the extent any portion of this Priority is withheld under Exemption 7(A), the agency is requested to identify the specific pending or prospective enforcement proceeding the disclosure would harm, consistent with NLRB v. Robbins Tire & Rubber Co., 437 U.S. 214 (1978).
Priority 9 — Communications between Treasury and Chevron concerning offtakers
All communications (as defined in Part II) between any custodian at Treasury — specifically including the Director of OFAC, OFAC Licensing, OFAC Office of Global Targeting, OFAC Chief Counsel, the Under Secretary for Terrorism and Financial Intelligence, and senior advisors to the Secretary — and any officer or employee of Chevron Corporation, any outside counsel or lobbyist representing Chevron, or any consultant acting on Chevron's behalf, concerning the identity, screening, approval, or compliance of the offtaker(s) for PdVSA's share of crude from the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures, the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or the Petroboscán offtake arrangement.

Priority 10 — License terms concerning offtakers
A complete, unredacted copy of any provision of the July 2025 specific license (and any amendments, side letters, attestations, reporting conditions, or implementing instruments) addressing offtaker selection, offtaker screening, approval of trading counterparties, or restrictions on the identity of buyers, traders, or end-users of PdVSA's share of crude.

IV. Anticipated exemption claims and requester's position
Foreseeable harm. 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(8)(A)(i)(I) requires the agency to articulate a "reasonably foreseeable" harm for each withholding. Reporters Comm. for Freedom of the Press v. FBI, 3 F.4th 350 (D.C. Cir. 2021).

Segregability. 5 U.S.C. § 552(b) requires release of "[a]ny reasonably segregable portion of a record." Factual material is to be released even within otherwise deliberative documents, and a Vaughn index is requested for any record withheld in full.

Exemption 3 / IEEPA. Section 1702(c) of IEEPA (50 U.S.C. § 1702(c)) protects information submitted to the President in connection with IEEPA programs; it does not by its terms immunize the agency's own decisional documents, communications with a licensee, or screening records concerning a third-party offtaker.

Exemption 4. Information about Shineful Energy's status as offtaker, the Shineful-Carretero relationship, and OFAC's screening of either, is not information "obtained from" Chevron within the meaning of Food Marketing Institute v. Argus Leader Media, 588 U.S. 427 (2019), and the relevant facts have been substantially disclosed by Treasury itself in connection with the December 2025 Carretero designation. If any submitter notice is provided to Chevron under Executive Order 12600, the requester requests notice and an opportunity to be heard.

Exemption 5. Exemption 5 does not protect factual material; communications reflecting adopted reasoning or final agency position (NLRB v. Sears, 421 U.S. 132 (1975)); or material as to which any deliberative concerns have dissipated. The Carretero designation is a final agency action, and post-decisional materials concerning it are not deliberative.

Exemption 7(A). Categorical withholding is insufficient; the agency must identify a specific pending or prospective proceeding. NLRB v. Robbins Tire & Rubber Co., 437 U.S. 214 (1978).

Exemption 7(E). To the extent the agency invokes 7(E) for sanctions-screening "techniques and procedures," the requester notes that release of the results of screening as applied to Mr. Carretero and the Shineful Entities does not disclose the techniques themselves and is not within the exemption's scope.

V. Search adequacy
The search should be "reasonably calculated to uncover all relevant documents." Weisberg v. DOJ, 745 F.2d 1476 (D.C. Cir. 1984). It should include:

The .gov email accounts of the custodians, plus any personal email accounts or personal devices used by those custodians to conduct agency business during the relevant period;
Text/SMS, iMessage, Signal, WhatsApp, Microsoft Teams, and any other messaging platform used by those custodians for agency business;
OFAC's internal databases including the SDN List management system, OFAC's licensing case-management system, and OFAC's Office of Global Targeting working files;
Calendar entries, voicemails, hard-copy records, and records held by contractors, detailees, or special government employees with relevant portfolios.
If the agency claims that any portion returns "no responsive records," the requester requests a declaration describing the search methodology, the systems and accounts searched, the search terms applied (including but not limited to "Shineful," "Carretero," "Carretero Mendoza," "Petroboscán," "Petroboscan," "offtaker," "offtake," "lifter," "lifting," and "Maduro-Flores"), and the custodians queried.

The agency is requested to take immediate steps to preserve all potentially responsive records, including ephemeral and auto-deleting messages, pending completion of this request.

VI. Format of production
Production in native format with metadata intact (.msg / .eml for email; .pdf only where originally PDF; native Office formats for native Office documents). For text messages and chat records, production in a structured export preserving sender, recipient, timestamp, and thread. Rolling production beginning with Priority 1.

For redacted records, please cite the specific exemption next to each redaction and provide a Vaughn index for any record withheld in full.

VII. Fee waiver and expedited processing
Fee waiver. Pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii) and 31 C.F.R. § 1.7. The records sought go directly to whether Treasury's licensing scheme has, in operation, channeled hundreds of millions of dollars in oil proceeds to a person Treasury itself has now designated as a Specially Designated National for ties to the Maduro-Flores family. Disclosure is "likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations or activities of the government" and is not primarily in my commercial interest. I [will publish / will share with affiliated journalists / am affiliated with the news media as defined in 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II)]. [Insert two to three sentences specifying your dissemination capacity.]

In the alternative, please treat me as a "representative of the news media" for fee purposes. Please contact me before assessing fees in excess of $25, and please provide an itemized estimate rather than a categorical assertion of burden.

Expedited processing. Pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E) and 31 C.F.R. § 1.5(e). There is "[a]n urgency to inform the public concerning actual or alleged Federal Government activity" because (a) the offtake arrangement remains operative; (b) Treasury's own December 2025 designation establishes that the principal beneficiary of the arrangement is a person sanctioned for ties to the Maduro regime; and (c) congressional and judicial deliberation on Venezuela policy is ongoing and will be informed by the records sought.

I certify under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E)(vi) that the foregoing statements made in support of expedited processing are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

VIII. Procedural matters
I ask that the agency:

Acknowledge receipt within ten working days and assign a tracking number.
Inform me immediately if any portion is being referred to another agency or component.
Confer with me before deeming any portion improperly described or unduly burdensome.
Treat the ten Priorities above as severable, so that production of Priority 1 records is not delayed by the agency's processing of subsequent Priorities.
Make a determination on expedited processing within the 10-calendar-day statutory window under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E)(ii)(I).
If the agency has not made a substantive determination within 20 working days, I intend to treat the lapse as a constructive denial and pursue all available administrative and judicial remedies, including appeal to the Treasury FOIA Appeals Officer and, where appropriate, suit under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(B).

Thank you for your attention to this request.

Sincerely,

Jessica M Kravitz

All records, from any date through July 31, 2025, in OFAC's possession (including OFAC's Office of Global Targeting, Licensing, Compliance and Enforcement, and Chief Counsel) referring or relating to Ramón Carretero, the Shineful Entities, or either's relationship to PdVSA, the Government of Venezuela, or the Maduro-Flores family. This Priority is intended to capture pre-existing intelligence, screening hits, prior license-application screening, prior enforcement referrals, and any analysis identifying Carretero or Shineful as risks before the issuance of the July 2025 Chevron specific license.

Priority 2 — The Carretero designation file
All records supporting OFAC's December 2025 designation of Mr. Carretero, including the action memorandum, evidentiary package, attribution analysis, redacted SDN listing rationale, any inter-agency concurrences, and any communications between OFAC and the Department of Justice, the Department of State, or the intelligence community concerning the designation.

Priority 3 — Identification of Shineful as offtaker
All records reflecting (a) when and how OFAC first became aware that the Shineful Entities had been awarded contracts to export PdVSA's share of crude from the Petroboscán field or any other Chevron-PdVSA joint venture; (b) any communications between OFAC and Chevron, between OFAC and PdVSA-adjacent persons, or among OFAC components concerning the identity of the offtaker(s) for PdVSA's share of the crude; and (c) any approval, no-action position, or interpretive guidance issued by OFAC concerning the offtaker arrangement.

Priority 4 — Chevron compliance submissions referencing Shineful
All compliance reports, attestations, KYC submissions, due-diligence files, beneficial-ownership disclosures, and other records submitted by or on behalf of Chevron Corporation pursuant to the July 2025 specific license (or any predecessor or successor license) that identify or reference the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, the Petroboscán offtake arrangement, or any trading firm awarded PdVSA's share of crude.

Priority 5 — OFAC due diligence on the offtaker arrangement
All internal OFAC due-diligence files, SDN-screening records, Worldcheck or commercial-database queries, beneficial-ownership analyses, and risk assessments concerning the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or the offtaker(s) selected for PdVSA's share of crude from the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures since July 1, 2025.

Priority 6 — Vessel, cargo, and shipping records
All records — including vessel-tracking data, shipping nominations, lifting schedules, bill-of-lading copies, AIS/EE records, and reports submitted by Chevron pursuant to license conditions — identifying any vessel or tanker that has loaded crude attributable to the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures since July 1, 2025, together with the identified consignee, end-buyer, and destination port. Records referencing tankers associated with the Shineful Entities or Mr. Carretero (including the tanker reportedly seized by U.S. authorities in December 2025) are specifically included.

Priority 7 — Records concerning destination and payment
Records sufficient to show (a) the destination jurisdictions for crude exported by the Shineful Entities under the offtake arrangements at issue (including but not limited to exports to the People's Republic of China); (b) the form and channels of payment for that crude, including any reports, intelligence, or analysis concerning the use of cryptocurrency or alternative payment mechanisms; and (c) the destination of proceeds.

Priority 8 — Enforcement records
All records concerning any enforcement action, investigation, license revocation, cease-and-desist, blocking notice, or referral to the Department of Justice related to the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or any vessel or transaction associated with either, including the December 2025 tanker seizure and any related forfeiture proceedings.

To the extent any portion of this Priority is withheld under Exemption 7(A), the agency is requested to identify the specific pending or prospective enforcement proceeding the disclosure would harm, consistent with NLRB v. Robbins Tire & Rubber Co., 437 U.S. 214 (1978).
Priority 9 — Communications between Treasury and Chevron concerning offtakers
All communications (as defined in Part II) between any custodian at Treasury — specifically including the Director of OFAC, OFAC Licensing, OFAC Office of Global Targeting, OFAC Chief Counsel, the Under Secretary for Terrorism and Financial Intelligence, and senior advisors to the Secretary — and any officer or employee of Chevron Corporation, any outside counsel or lobbyist representing Chevron, or any consultant acting on Chevron's behalf, concerning the identity, screening, approval, or compliance of the offtaker(s) for PdVSA's share of crude from the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures, the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or the Petroboscán offtake arrangement.

Priority 10 — License terms concerning offtakers
A complete, unredacted copy of any provision of the July 2025 specific license (and any amendments, side letters, attestations, reporting conditions, or implementing instruments) addressing offtaker selection, offtaker screening, approval of trading counterparties, or restrictions on the identity of buyers, traders, or end-users of PdVSA's share of crude.

Foreseeable harm. 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(8)(A)(i)(I) requires the agency to articulate a "reasonably foreseeable" harm for each withholding. Reporters Comm. for Freedom of the Press v. FBI, 3 F.4th 350 (D.C. Cir. 2021).

Sears, 421 U.S. 132 (1975)); or material as to which any deliberative concerns have dissipated. The search should be "reasonably calculated to uncover all relevant documents." Weisberg v. DOJ, 745 F.2d 1476 (D.C. Cir. 1984). It should include:

The .gov email accounts of the custodians, plus any personal email accounts or personal devices used by those custodians to conduct agency business during the relevant period;
Text/SMS, iMessage, Signal, WhatsApp, Microsoft Teams, and any other messaging platform used by those custodians for agency business;
OFAC's internal databases including the SDN List management system, OFAC's licensing case-management system, and OFAC's Office of Global Targeting working files;
Calendar entries, voicemails, hard-copy records, and records held by contractors, detailees, or special government employees with relevant portfolios.

For redacted records, please cite the specific exemption next to each redaction and provide a Vaughn index for any record withheld in full.

Fee waiver. Pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii) and 31 C.F.R. § 1.7. The records sought go directly to whether Treasury's licensing scheme has, in operation, channeled hundreds of millions of dollars in oil proceeds to a person Treasury itself has now designated as a Specially Designated National for ties to the Maduro-Flores family. Disclosure is "likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations or activities of the government" and is not primarily in my commercial interest. VIII. Procedural matters
I ask that the agency:

Acknowledge receipt within ten working days and assign a tracking number.
Inform me immediately if any portion is being referred to another agency or component.
Confer with me before deeming any portion improperly described or unduly burdensome.
Treat the ten Priorities above as severable, so that production of Priority 1 records is not delayed by the agency's processing of subsequent Priorities.
Make a determination on expedited processing within the 10-calendar-day statutory window under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E)(ii)(I).
If the agency has not made a substantive determination within 20 working days, I intend to treat the lapse as a constructive denial and pursue all available administrative and judicial remedies, including appeal to the Treasury FOIA Appeals Officer and, where appropriate, suit under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(B).

Thank you for your attention to this request.

Sincerely,

Jessica M Kravitz
